digest.top_line: PRIMARY2: PSMA-PET non-inferior to systematic biopsy for csPCa (12% vs 16%, diff −3.7%), avoiding biopsy in 49% of high-risk biopsy-naive men.
digest.tldr: Prostate carried the day's only signal. PRIMARY2, a phase 3 non-inferiority RCT, shows upfront PSMA-PET matches systematic biopsy for csPCa detection while sparing biopsy in nearly half of high-risk, biopsy-naive men. Australian, single-tracer, follow-up ongoing.

sites.intro: A diagnostic-pathway question, not a therapy result: can PSMA-PET front-run systematic biopsy in biopsy-naive men.

studies.name: PRIMARY2
studies.tldr: csPCa detection non-inferior with PSMA-PET vs systematic biopsy (12% vs 16%, diff −3.7%, 95% CI −8.9 to 1.5), and biopsy avoided in 49%.
details: 📊 Co-primary 1 (csPCa detection): 12% (39/331) PSMA-PET vs 16% (51/329) systematic biopsy, non-inferior
📐 Risk difference −3.7% (95% CI −8.9 to 1.5), p=0.0093; non-inferiority margin 10%
📊 Co-primary 2: PSMA-PET avoided biopsy in 49% (163/331), 95% CI 44-55%, p<0.0001 (vs 20% threshold)
🔍 Multicentre phase 3 non-inferiority RCT, 7 Australian sites, N=660, 1:1, ITT, no masking
🔍 Biopsy-naive men, high clinical risk, PI-RADS 2-3 MRI, PSA ≤20, ≤cT2

details.text: 📊 Post-biopsy morbidity, PSMA-PET vs systematic biopsy (of those biopsied)

details: 🔗 Extends PRIMARY (Emmett, 2024): PSMA-PET+MRI improved detection in equivocal/negative MRI; PRIMARY2 is the phase 3 non-inferiority test
⚠️ Single country (Australia) and one radiopharmaceutical ([68Ga]Ga-PSMA-11); no masking
⚠️ Follow-up ongoing; biopsy-avoided men not fully tracked, so long-term missed-csPCa rate unknown

verdict.rationale: Phase 3 non-inferiority RCT met both co-primaries, extending the emerging PSMA-PET biopsy-triage pathway (PRIMARY). Australia-only, follow-up ongoing so long-term missed-cancer rate immature.
verdict.audience: Biopsy-naive men, high clinical risk, PSA ≤20, PI-RADS 2-3 MRI

studies.monday_clinic: In biopsy-naive men with high clinical risk but PI-RADS 2-3 MRI and PSA ≤20, PSMA-PET triage supports avoiding biopsy in roughly half without inferior csPCa detection; it does not extend to PI-RADS 4-5 lesions or PSA >20.

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Multicentre **phase 3 non-inferiority RCT** at 7 Australian hospitals, N=660, randomised 1:1, ITT, no masking. Enrolled March 2022 to Aug 2025; follow-up ongoing.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: Biopsy-naive men with clinical suspicion of significant prostate cancer, **PI-RADS 2 (335, 51%) or PI-RADS 3 (325, 49%)** MRI plus high clinical risk (PSA density >0.1, family history, abnormal DRE, BRCA, PSA >10, or PSA doubling time <36 months), **PSA ≤20, ≤cT2**. Median age 61, PSA 5.2, PSA density 0.13.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: **Co-primary:** proportion with clinically significant prostate cancer (Gleason 3+4 with ≥10% pattern 4 or higher) and proportion avoiding biopsy within 6 months. Non-inferiority margin 10%; biopsy-avoidance threshold 20%; analysed by ITT.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: **Both co-primaries met:** csPCa detection non-inferior and biopsy avoided in about half. Post-biopsy pain, haematuria, and haematospermia similar between arms.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents biopsy-naive men with high clinical risk but PI-RADS 2-3 MRI and PSA ≤20. Does not represent PI-RADS 4-5 lesions, PSA >20, or previously biopsied men.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: No masking of participants or investigators. Single country (Australia) and one radiopharmaceutical ([68Ga]Ga-PSMA-11), limiting generalisability. Follow-up ongoing, so durability of safe biopsy avoidance (delayed csPCa) is not yet established.

open_questions: Long-term missed csPCa rate in biopsy-avoided men

related_trials.brief_title: PSMA PET Combined With MRI for the Detection of PCa
related_trials.brief_summary: This is an open label, longitudinal Phase 3 study of prostate specific membrane antigen (PSMA) positron emission tomography (PET) combined with magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) compared to standard of care (SOC) for the detection of prostate cancer (PCa).
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:

1. Male, at least 18 years old.
2. Have a clinical suspicion of PCa, and will undergo template biopsy, based on either: an initial MRI examination (PI-RADS 3-4) within 3 months (92 days) before enrollment, or no MRI evidence (PI-RADs 1-2) within 3 months (92 days) before…

related_trials.brief_title: Development and Validation of a Non-Invasive AI Diagnostic Model for Prostate Cancer Using Multiparametric MRI and PSMA PET/CT
related_trials.brief_summary: Prostate cancer is one of the most common malignancies in men. Currently, due to the limited diagnostic accuracy of existing imaging tests, there is a risk of missed diagnosis or unnecessary prostate biopsy. This study aims to develop and validate a non-invasive artificial intelligence (AI) diagnostic model using two advanced imaging techniques: multiparametric MRI (mpMRI) and PSMA PET/CT. By integrating information from both imaging modalities, the AI model is expected to improve the…
related_trials.eligibility_brief:

1. Age ≥ 18 years.
2. ECOG performance status 0-2.
3. Life expectancy \> 6 months.

related_trials.brief_title: Virtual Biopsy of Prostate Cancer Using PSMA PET and AI
related_trials.brief_summary:
Prostate cancer is the most common type of cancer in Norwegian men, but many tumors are slow-growing and do not require treatment. Today, MRI is good at detecting suspicious lesions, yet it cannot reliably distinguish aggressive tumors from low-grade ones. As a result, many men undergo repeated invasive biopsies. New PET tracers targeting PSMA improve tumor localization and may correlate with cancer aggressiveness, offering potential for better assessment.

This project aims to develop a method…

related_trials.brief_title: Comparing PSMA PET/CT and MRI-RSI for Finding and Outlining Tumors Inside the Prostate in Men With Newly Diagnosed Prostate Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary:
The detection and delineation of the intraprostatic tumor burden plays a crucial role in the personalized treatment of primary prostate cancer. The current gold standard multiparametric magnetic resonance imaging (mpMRI) is used to guide targeted prostate biopsies for initial diagnostic work up and for definitive focal dose-escalated radiotherapy in intermediate and high-risk prostate cancer patients.

However, mpMRI might underestimate the tumor volume and manual gross tumor volume delineation…

* Newly diagnosed and histologically confirmed primary prostate cancer
* NCCNv4.2024 risk groups: unfavorable intermediate risk, high-risk and very high-risk
* Males, age ≥ 18 years
* ECOG performance status 0-2
* Estimated life expectancy ≥ 5 years
* PSMA-PET/CT-hybrid imaging…
related_trials.relevance_phrase: 18F-PSMA1007 vs MRI for newly-dx tumor detection

related_trials.brief_summary: The goal of this study to determine if using the (18)F-rhPSMA-7.3 PET-CT scan can improve the ability to spot serious prostate cancer in patients who have uncertain MRI results. These uncertain results include MRI results that showed a PIRADS 2 or PIRADS 3 area on the prostate. The investigators want to find out if this new test is better to detect prostate cancer than just doing a biopsy based on MRI alone.

papers.title: Effect of [68Ga]Ga-PSMA-11 PET-CT in the diagnosis of prostate cancer in men with equivocal or clinically high-risk non-suspicious findings on multiparametric MRI (PRIMARY2): a multicentre, non-inferiority, phase 3, randomised controlled trial
